title: any + sound narrow-only via case
manager: An `any` escape hatch for when a value's type genuinely isn't known ahead of time — but narrowing out of `any` is only allowed soundly, through a `case` that checks, never by a bare cast. So `any` gives you the flexibility to handle genuinely dynamic data without giving up the guarantee that once you've used a value at a type, it really was that type.
cs: `any` top type; sound narrowing only via checked case (W6.41); no unchecked downcast; keeps the type system sound despite the escape hatch.
